purpose: Meta-framework establishing that downstream analytical precision cannot exceed upstream measurement precision, constraining the entire KB's confidence structure
components: NAIRU 95% CI of ±150bp sets an upper bound on precision of any NAIRU-dependent threshold
NFP ±130K CI limits precision of any employment-level-dependent trigger
Confidence propagation rule: downstream concept confidence ≤ min(upstream confidence × relationship confidence)
KB violates this throughout with downstream concepts at higher confidence than antecedents
current_assessment: The KB specifies margin triggers at 8-9% and ULC thresholds at 3.5% while acknowledging NAIRU uncertainty of ±150bp — a logical contradiction. This is a mathematical constraint, not a debatable interpretation.
limitations: The framework identifies the problem but does not specify how to optimally downgrade dependent concepts
Some thresholds may be empirically robust despite noisy upstream inputs
The framework may be overly conservative if applied mechanically to all causal chains
